zh-CN.title: 用 AI 做个人知识雷达
zh-CN.excerpt: 把资讯、论文、产品更新和自己的兴趣主题放进同一套筛选流程，减少低价值浏览。
zh-CN.author: 许望
body: 个人知识雷达的重点不是抓取更多内容，而是更快判断哪些内容值得进入下一步阅读。
可以先把来源分成长期关注、偶尔巡检和临时搜索三类，再用标签记录主题、可信度和行动价值。
当后台支持知识源以后，这套流程就可以变成自动化订阅、摘要和待读队列。

en-US.title: Using AI as a personal knowledge radar
en-US.excerpt: Put news, papers, product updates, and personal interests into one filtering workflow to reduce low-value browsing.
en-US.author: Noah Xu
body: A personal knowledge radar is not about collecting more. It is about deciding faster what deserves real attention.
Start by grouping sources into daily signals, occasional patrols, and one-off searches, then tag topic, trust, and action value.
When the admin app supports knowledge sources, this workflow can become automated subscriptions, summaries, and reading queues.

zh-CN.title: 把音乐当作记忆索引
zh-CN.excerpt: 一首歌不只是音频文件，也可能是一段时间、一个地点和一种情绪的入口。
zh-CN.author: 林屿声
body: 收藏音乐时，如果只保存歌名，很快会失去上下文。加上时间、场景和一句短注释，记忆会更容易回来。
博客里的音乐模块可以不只是播放器，还可以是个人生活片段的索引。
当上传功能稳定后，音乐、文章和分享页之间可以形成更自然的个人表达链路。

en-US.title: Music as an index for memory
en-US.excerpt: A song can be more than an audio file. It can become an entrance to a time, a place, and a mood.
en-US.author: Mira Lin
body: If you only save a song title, context disappears quickly. Add a time, scene, and short note and memory becomes easier to recover.
A music module in a blog can be more than a player. It can become an index of personal moments.
Once uploads are stable, music, articles, and sharing pages can form a more natural personal expression flow.

zh-CN.title: 小后台系统也需要清晰边界
zh-CN.excerpt: 哪怕只是博客后台，也应该把登录、内容、上传和公开展示分成清楚的模块。
zh-CN.author: Admin
body: 后台系统最容易变成一个大表单，但真正可维护的后台应该先明确模块边界。
文章、音乐、用户和分享配置各自有不同的数据生命周期，也应该有不同的操作入口。
当前项目已经把公开读取和带 token 写入拆开，后续接数据库时会轻松很多。

en-US.title: Small admin systems still need clear boundaries
en-US.excerpt: Even a blog admin app should separate login, content, uploads, and public display into clear modules.
en-US.author: Admin
body: Admin apps easily become one giant form, but maintainable systems start with module boundaries.
Articles, music, users, and sharing settings each have different data lifecycles and deserve different entry points.
This project already separates public reads from token-protected writes, which will help when a real database arrives.

zh-CN.title: 设计 token 的实用地图
zh-CN.excerpt: 颜色、间距、圆角和阴影不只是变量命名，更是产品体验保持一致的约定。
zh-CN.author: 周澈
body: 设计 token 的价值在于让视觉决策可以被复用，而不是让每个页面重新选择一次。
博客这类内容产品尤其适合用 token 管理主题，因为阅读体验对颜色和层级非常敏感。
当多套皮肤共用一组语义变量时，新增主题会比逐页改样式可靠得多。

en-US.title: A practical map for design tokens
en-US.excerpt: Color, spacing, radius, and shadow tokens are not only variable names. They are agreements for consistency.
en-US.author: Theo Zhou
body: The value of design tokens is making visual decisions reusable instead of choosing again on every page.
Content products are a good fit for tokenized themes because reading comfort depends heavily on color and hierarchy.
When multiple skins share semantic variables, adding a new theme is much safer than editing styles page by page.

zh-CN.title: 周末写作的三个小仪式
zh-CN.excerpt: 固定时间、固定入口和固定收尾动作，会让写作从临时兴起变成可持续习惯。
zh-CN.author: 韩以安
body: 写作习惯不一定需要很宏大的计划，反而可以从几个稳定的小动作开始。
例如周六早上只整理素材，周日下午只完成一段正文，晚上只做标题和摘要。
把流程拆小之后，后台的草稿、审核和发布状态就不只是字段，而是写作节奏的一部分。

en-US.title: Three small rituals for weekend writing
en-US.excerpt: Fixed time, fixed entrance, and fixed closing actions can turn occasional writing into a sustainable habit.
en-US.author: Iris Han
body: A writing habit does not need a grand plan. It can begin with a few stable actions.
For example, collect notes on Saturday morning, draft one section on Sunday afternoon, and polish title plus excerpt at night.
Once the flow is smaller, draft, review, and published states become part of the writing rhythm instead of mere fields.

zh-CN.title: 多语言博客上线前要检查什么
zh-CN.excerpt: 语言文件、路由标题、日期格式、空状态和搜索行为，都需要在真实页面里验证。
zh-CN.author: 许望
body: 多语言不是把文本翻译一遍就结束，真正麻烦的是所有状态都要有对应语言。
导航、按钮、空结果、错误提示和表单反馈都应该来自语言文件，而不是散落在组件里。
上线前最好用中英文各走一遍核心流程，尤其是搜索、登录和内容详情页。

en-US.title: What to check before shipping a multilingual blog
en-US.excerpt: Language files, route labels, date formats, empty states, and search behavior all need real-page verification.
en-US.author: Noah Xu
body: Multilingual support does not end after translating text. Every state needs a language-aware version.
Navigation, buttons, empty results, errors, and form feedback should come from language files instead of scattered components.
Before shipping, walk through the main flows in both languages, especially search, login, and detail pages.

zh-CN.title: 公开数字花园不必完美
zh-CN.excerpt: 个人网站可以容纳正在成长的想法，而不是只展示已经打磨到极致的作品。
zh-CN.author: 林屿声
body: 如果每篇文章都必须像正式出版物，个人博客很容易停更。
数字花园的好处是允许内容有不同成熟度：片段、草稿、札记和完整文章都能共存。
关键是给读者清楚的状态提示，让他们知道这是探索、记录还是结论。

en-US.title: A public digital garden does not need to be perfect
en-US.excerpt: A personal site can hold growing ideas instead of only displaying highly polished work.
en-US.author: Mira Lin
body: If every article must feel like a finished publication, a personal blog becomes hard to maintain.
The benefit of a digital garden is allowing different maturity levels: fragments, drafts, notes, and essays can coexist.
The key is giving readers clear status signals so they know whether they are seeing exploration, record, or conclusion.

zh-CN.title: 响应式布局要先考虑信息密度
zh-CN.excerpt: PC 和 H5 不只是列数不同，真正不同的是用户一次能承受的信息量。
zh-CN.author: 周澈
body: 响应式布局不能只靠断点切换列数，还要考虑每个视口下用户最需要先看到什么。
例如音乐卡片在桌面可以四列展示，在移动端则需要压缩图片高度和操作按钮。
好的响应式设计会让内容变紧凑，但不会让操作变得难点。

en-US.title: Responsive layout starts with information density
en-US.excerpt: Desktop and mobile are not only different column counts. They carry different amounts of attention.
en-US.author: Theo Zhou
body: Responsive layout is not just switching columns at breakpoints. It begins with what users need to see first.
For example, music cards can use four columns on desktop while mobile needs shorter media and compact actions.
Good responsive design makes content denser without making controls harder to use.

zh-CN.title: 先定接口契约，再换数据库
zh-CN.excerpt: 即使暂时用内存数据，也可以先把前后端边界设计清楚，后续迁移成本会低很多。
zh-CN.author: Admin
body: 项目早期没有数据库并不可怕，可怕的是前端直接依赖临时数据结构。
只要接口路径、请求体和响应体稳定，后端从内存换成数据库时前台改动会很少。
当前博客项目的文章、音乐和登录接口已经可以作为后续数据库设计的第一版契约。

en-US.title: Define API contracts before replacing storage
en-US.excerpt: Even with in-memory data, clear frontend and backend boundaries make future database migration cheaper.
en-US.author: Admin
body: Not having a database early is fine. The risky part is letting the frontend depend on temporary data structures.
If routes, request bodies, and response bodies are stable, moving from memory to a database requires fewer frontend changes.
The current article, music, and login APIs can become the first contract for future storage design.

zh-CN.title: 安静的后台界面更耐用
zh-CN.excerpt: 后台不是落地页，过度装饰会降低扫描效率，清晰分组和稳定控件更重要。
zh-CN.author: 周澈
body: 管理后台的核心用户通常会重复使用同一批功能，所以界面应该服务扫描和决策。
卡片、表单和导航都应该保持稳定，让用户不需要重新理解每个模块。
适度的颜色可以帮助区分状态，但不要让后台变成展示型页面。

en-US.title: Quiet dashboard design lasts longer
en-US.excerpt: An admin app is not a landing page. Heavy decoration hurts scanning; clear grouping and stable controls matter more.
en-US.author: Theo Zhou
body: Admin users repeat the same workflows, so the interface should support scanning and decisions.
Cards, forms, and navigation should stay stable so users do not have to relearn each module.
Color can help distinguish states, but the admin surface should not become a showcase page.

zh-CN.title: 把灵感留在本地，再慢慢生长
zh-CN.excerpt: 一个更轻的写作工作流：先记录未经修饰的想法，再把它们整理成可以分享的文章。
zh-CN.author: 林予安
body: 很多好想法刚出现时都很粗糙。把它们先放进本地草稿，而不是立刻塞进正式发布流程，会给思考留下更多余地。
这套流程的关键不是工具复杂度，而是节奏：快速记录、定期回看、把相近的片段聚成主题，再决定哪些值得继续写。
当博客内容由后台维护时，前台只负责展示与阅读体验，内容生产链路会清楚很多。

en-US.title: Keep the spark local, then let it grow
en-US.excerpt: A lighter writing workflow: capture rough thoughts first, then shape them into essays worth sharing.
en-US.author: Mira Lin
body: Most good ideas are messy at first. Keeping them in drafts before publishing gives the thinking more room.
The important part is rhythm: capture quickly, review regularly, group related fragments, then decide what deserves a full essay.
When content is managed by the backend, the web app can focus on presentation and reading experience.

zh-CN.title: 前端可观测性，从真实路径开始
zh-CN.excerpt: 埋点、性能指标和错误追踪只有放回用户路径里，才能真正解释体验为何顺滑或卡顿。
zh-CN.author: 许望
body: 单独看一个错误日志，很难判断它对体验造成了多大影响。把它放回用户路径，问题才有优先级。
前端可观测性应当同时关注页面性能、交互响应、资源加载和业务关键路径。

en-US.title: Frontend observability starts with real paths
en-US.excerpt: Analytics, performance metrics, and errors only become useful when they are tied back to user journeys.
en-US.author: Noah Xu
body: A single error log rarely explains how much the experience was damaged. Put it back into a user path and priority becomes clearer.
Frontend observability should cover page performance, interaction latency, asset loading, and business-critical journeys.

zh-CN.title: 信息密度与呼吸感并不冲突
zh-CN.excerpt: 面向长期使用的界面，应当让内容更容易扫描，而不是把每个模块都做成展示橱窗。
zh-CN.author: 周澈
body: 信息密度不是把所有东西挤在一起。真正有用的密度，是让用户在短时间内读到更多有效关系。
对博客而言，列表、摘要、分类和阅读时间都应该服务于判断：这篇文章是否值得现在点开。

en-US.title: Density and breathing room can coexist
en-US.excerpt: Interfaces used every day should make content easier to scan, not turn every module into a display case.
en-US.author: Theo Zhou
body: Density is not the act of squeezing everything together. Useful density lets readers understand more relationships in less time.
For a blog, lists, excerpts, categories, and reading time all help with one question: is this article worth opening now.

zh-CN.title: 团队知识库应该像一座小城市
zh-CN.excerpt: 好的知识系统不只有入口，还要有路标、街区和可以偶遇旧想法的开放空间。
zh-CN.author: 韩以宁
body: 知识库最怕只有入口，没有道路。用户进得去，却不知道应该往哪里走。
像城市一样组织知识，意味着有清晰街区，也允许读者在旧内容之间自然穿行。

en-US.title: A team knowledge base should feel like a city
en-US.excerpt: Good knowledge systems have more than entrances: they need signs, districts, and room for accidental discovery.
en-US.author: Iris Han
body: A knowledge base with only an entrance quickly becomes tiring. People can get in, but they do not know where to go next.
Organizing knowledge like a city means creating clear districts while still allowing readers to wander between older ideas.

zh-CN.title: 发布说明也可以是产品体验的一部分
zh-CN.excerpt: 当变更说明不再只是罗列功能，用户会更清楚地理解一次更新对自己意味着什么。
zh-CN.author: 许望
body: 发布说明不是产品之外的附属品。它是用户理解变化、建立信任和继续使用的重要线索。
好的说明会讲清楚变化背后的意图，而不是只把功能点机械地列出来。

en-US.title: Release notes can be part of the product
en-US.excerpt: When updates explain intent instead of listing features, users understand what changed for them.
en-US.author: Noah Xu
body: Release notes are not outside the product. They help users understand change, build trust, and keep using the thing.
Strong notes explain intent instead of mechanically listing features.
